papers.title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
papers.abstract: Language model agents are increasingly being used to automate complicated tasks in digital environments. We introduce SWE-agent, a system that facilitates LM agents to autonomously use computers to solve software engineering tasks via a custom agent-computer interface (ACI). SWE-agent achieves state-of-the-art performance on SWE-bench and HumanEvalFix.

papers.title: AGILE: A Novel Reinforcement Learning Framework of LLM Agents
papers.abstract: We introduce AGILE (AGent that Interacts and Learns from Environments), an RL framework for LLM agents that use memory, tools, expert consultation, and reflection. AGILE agents trained with PPO outperform GPT-4 agents on ProductQA, MedMCQA, and HotPotQA.

papers.title: ToolkenGPT: Augmenting Frozen Language Models with Massive Tools via Tool Embeddings
papers.abstract: We propose ToolkenGPT, which represents each external tool as a token with a learned embedding so frozen LLMs can call massive tool sets like generating words. ToolkenGPT improves numerical reasoning, knowledge QA, and embodied plan generation without fine-tuning the base LLM.

authors.name: Zhen Wang
authors.affiliation: Mohamed bin Zayed University of Artificial Intelligence

papers.title: PIVOT-R: Primitive-Driven Waypoint-Aware World Model for Robotic Manipulation
papers.abstract: PIVOT-R is a primitive-driven waypoint-aware world model for language-guided robotic manipulation. It predicts task-relevant waypoints with a Waypoint-aware World Model and uses an asynchronous hierarchical executor to reduce computational redundancy while improving performance on SeaWave.

papers.title: Learning to Bridge the Gap: Efficient Novelty Recovery with Planning and Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: We propose learning a bridge policy via reinforcement learning to recover from environment novelties during deployment. A CallPlanner action hands control back to the planner once the bridge policy resolves the failure, enabling efficient adaptation across simulated robotic domains.

papers.title: Generalized Protein Pocket Generation with Prior-Informed Flow Matching
papers.abstract: PocketFlow is a flow-matching generative model for protein pocket design that incorporates protein-ligand interaction priors. It improves binding affinity and structural validity across small-molecule, peptide, and RNA ligand modalities for structure-based drug design.

authors.name: Zaixi Zhang
authors.affiliation: University of Science and Technology of China

authors.name: Qi Liu
authors.affiliation: University of Science and Technology of China

papers.title: Multi-Scale Representation Learning for Protein Fitness Prediction
papers.abstract: We introduce S3F, a multi-scale protein representation learning framework integrating sequence, structure, and surface features for zero-shot fitness prediction. S3F achieves state-of-the-art results on the ProteinGym benchmark spanning more than 200 deep mutational scanning assays.
